target.title: fix(cli): clear provider auth warm state on exit
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear process-current provider auth warm state during short-lived CLI teardown.
- Terminate any active provider-auth warm worker after local JSON agent completion instead of leaving cleanup to process shutdown.
- Add focused CLI exit coverage for `openclaw agent --local --json`.

Fixes #92464

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `openclaw agent --local --json` can emit its final JSON payload while provider-auth warm state still owns process-current worker cleanup. The CLI now clears provider auth warm state in the same teardown path that already disposes proxy, agent harness, and memory managers.

**Environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Verification used focused CLI lifecycle tests only; no live provider credentials or external model calls were used.

**Command run after the patch:**

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Observed result:**

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  92 passed (92)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.45s
git diff --check produced no output
```

## Notes

The full Docker reproduction with a 10k memory corpus from the issue was not run here. This patch targets the source-level CLI lifecycle gap by exercising the short-lived local JSON agent path directly.
target.author: mushuiyu886
